metrics.narrative: The stated goals are efficiency gains and total cost of ownership (TCO) reduction across the National Holding group, voiced directly by the buyer on the 8 Oct 2025 call: 'reduce the total cost of ownership somehow... all that feature benefit from the AI tools integrated with the system' (Avoma call, 8 Oct 2025). On 30 Mar 2026 the deal owner built a draft ROI calculator and shared it with Jihad Ibrahim, proposing to build it out jointly and co-present to the CFO to help secure budget approval (Next_Step_History__c, 30 Mar 2026), but no later Next Step entry confirms this ROI case was finished, shared with, or reviewed by a CFO. Metrics exist directionally but remain unquantified and unvalidated by the economic buyer.

champion.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim, Group Director of Procurement, is the only consistently engaged buyer contact across ten months of activity (first engaged 2025-09-09, most recently replying by email 2026-07-03) and is named as champion in MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15). However, the deal owner's own note flags him as aspirational, not secured: 'DQ: Want to try and turn Jihad into a champion' (Next_Step_History__c, 2026-01-23). He has not been shown advocating to a CFO or escalating internally beyond submitting a 'procurement strategy' for management review; Chad Bautista, the named Executive Sponsor contact role, has never surfaced in any read evidence.

competition.narrative: Coupa and SAP Ariba were named directly by the buyer as the original formal-evaluation shortlist at the Abu Dhabi World Tour event (2025-09-09), with Zycus added afterward as a third option. On the 2025-10-08 call, the deal owner positioned Zycus 'closer to Coupa' (citing Coupa's UI strength but weak CLM and sourcing) and characterized SAP Ariba as 'yesterday's technology... massively underinvested,' claiming a 7-8 year AI head start over both. This remains the most recent competitive discussion on record (9 months old); there is no evidence of how Zycus stands now that the formal RFP response has been submitted (2026-06-10) — flagged as a gap below.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer directly named group-wide procurement fragmentation and cost inefficiency as the driver: 'we are interested to move on to the next step... how this will help us in our strategic goals of achieving efficiency of our work, reduce the total cost of ownership' (Avoma call, 2025-10-08). The 30 Oct 2025 onsite additionally surfaced execution-level pain: the buyer 'has a lot of gaps in data for the QIT (including no idea what their revenue or spend is...not unusual for a ME private co)' (Next_Step_History__c, 2025-10-30), reinforcing a lack of centralized group visibility.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifact (MSA, SOW, Order Form) has been discussed anywhere in the record; the deal has not progressed past RFP submission and evaluation. This is an appropriate gap for a Formal Evaluation-stage deal that has not yet reached vendor selection.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 2026-05-15) states Jihad Ibrahim is the decision maker but that senior approval from a CFO is required, with the CFO's identity marked 'TBC.' Account-level contacts list two possible group CFOs, Fareed Bilbeis and Saed Arekat, but neither is a contact role on this opportunity nor has appeared by name in any call or Next Step entry. Budget approval was first flagged pending on 23 Jan 2026 ('submitted to management for their review and approval') and remained unresolved as of 25 Mar 2026 ('still underway as we work on securing budget approval'), with no update since. The economic buyer is named at the role level but never directly engaged.

decision_process.narrative: The process moved from an informal shortlist (Coupa and SAP Ariba named at the Abu Dhabi World Tour event, 2025-09-09) to a formal RFP: received 2026-05-07, deadline extended to 2026-06-10, and a response submitted that date with a discount retained for future negotiation. The buyer's stated process (8 Oct 2025 call) was proposal review → management decision → possible second implementation-partner comparison. No RFP evaluation outcome or vendor-selection date has been communicated in the 29 days since submission, so the process is confirmed in shape but stalled in its current step.

decision_criteria.narrative: Three criteria surfaced directly from the buyer on the 8 Oct 2025 call and are captured in MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15): group-wide benefit ('how this will help our group of companies'), efficiency/TCO reduction, and implementation-approach comparison (in-house vs. Big Four) alongside pricing structure. The buyer also asked pointed licensing questions during the same call ('Is it license based? How does your pricing structure work?'), confirming cost model is an active criterion. These criteria have held consistent through the formal RFP (received 2026-05-07, submitted 2026-06-10).

day_summary.overall: Jihad Ibrahim went on leave and put the deal on hold, replying only to confirm he'll pick things up when back in office, while on Zycus's side there's a fresh push to re-engage: new Regional VP Mohamad Alhakim was introduced to take ownership of the relationship and understand where the evaluation stands. Carl Kimball used the pause constructively, floating an in-person lunch/office visit when he's next in the region rather than pushing for immediate action. The ball is now in Jihad's court — deal is effectively paused until he returns from leave.

If Jihad leaves, is overruled, or goes silent (currently 16 days no reply), the deal stalls or dies.
